rulings.Case Title: MIDLAND CREDIT MANAGEMENT INC VS. ERLINDA SEVILLA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 9, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT ERLINDA SEVILLA's Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment. The motion is denied without prejudice. On April 29, 2013, the Court ordered the moving party to provide courtesy copies of its papers and warned that the failure to do so could result in the motion being denied. Defendant failed to provide the courtesy copies. Local Rule 2.6(B). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LVNV FUNDING LLC VS. SALLY WIATROLIK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 9, 2013, Line 15, PLAINTIFF LVNV FUNDING LLCS'S Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Granted in part and denied in part. Judgment debtor failed to show that any of the funds can be traced to worker's compensation payments deposited into her account. However, judgment debtor has shown that $2,485 of the funds levied upon are paid earnings. Paid earnings are 75% exempt under Code of Civ. Pro. section 704.070(b)(2). Accordingly, $1,863.75 shall be released to the judgment debtor. The remaining amount of $2,920.83 shall be released to the judgment creditor for payment toward the judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A. VS. REMEDIOS A. FERNANDEZ et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 9, 2013, Line 17, PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA), N.A.'S Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Denied. According to the Employer's Return, judgment debtor has a monthly gross income of $11,050.80 and therefore does not need all of her earnings for her support. The levying officer is directed to release any earnings held to the judgment creditor. rulings.Case Title: CAROLYN BENGTSON VS. SAN FRANCISCO SUPPLY MASTER, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Independant Psychiatric Evaluation With Dr. Mark Strassberg,
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 9, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT CORD INVESTMENT COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Independant Psychiatric Evaluation With Dr. Mark Strassberg. Granted. The psychiatric examination of Plaintiff by Dr. Strassberg shall consist of a three hour oral examination and the written true/false tests articulated in Defendant's motion (MMPI-2 and MCMI-III). Good cause exists because Plaintiff intends to introduce her own psychologist to testify on her behalf and Defendant should be able to produce its own expert to refute that testimony. The Court will sign the proposed Order re Motion to Compel IPE submitted with the moving papers. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JANNICKE KOLDERUP et al VS. OKLS, INC., DOING BUSINESS AS OKELL'S FIREPLACE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Olks, Inc. Doing Business As Okell'S Fireplace'S Motion To Compel Inspection And Testing Of Property; Request For An Award Of Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 9, 2013, Line 5, Defendant Olks, Inc. Doing Business As Okell'S Fireplace'S Motion To Compel Inspection And Testing Of Property; Request For An Award Of Sanctions. The motion to compel further inspection of property is denied. The relief sought by Defendant is not limited to inspecting or testing, but rather seeks permission to make modifications to Plaintiffs' home. Defendant has cited no authority under CCP section 2031.010(c) that would compel a party to allow the inspecting party to make modifications and recreate hazardous conditions such as those requested here. Further, the Court has considered the extent of the alterations Defendant proposes to make to Plaintiffs' home in order to attempt to recreate preexisting conditions and the uncertainty that the alterations will result in a test that is accurate, and balanced that against the possible prejudicial impact to Plaintiffs of recreating hazardous conditions in their home. Under the circumstances, the request for further inspection is unwarranted and is denied. No monetary sanctions are awarded. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCISCO VALENCIA et al VS. BNBUILDERS INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File X-Complt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 9, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT BNBT BUILDERS, INC DBA BNBUILDERS'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File CROSS-ComplAINt. The motion is denied without prejudice in light of the current trial date. Defendant is seeking to file a permissive cross-complaint, not a compulsory one. See CCP 426.30; CCP 428.10. Therefore, Silver Orgs. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94 does not apply to this motion. Granting leave to file the cross-complaint would prejudice plaintiff. Defendant can file an independent action for indemnity against Conco. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. DELTA AIR LINES, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 9, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT DELTA AIR LINES, INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Hearing required. Counsel are requested to be prepared to address 1. whether the Complaint for Civil Penalties and Injunction and Other Equitable Relief for Violation of B&P § 17200 (Unfair Competition Law) is preempted by the Airline Deregulation Act ("ADA"); 2. the status on appeal of National Federation of the Blind v. United Airlines, Inc. (2011 WL 1544524 ("National Federation"); 3. to what extent, if any, the Court should consider that the United States filed a statement of interest in National Federation that the ADA preempted state law claims relating to kiosks; 4. whether a state law designed to prevent unfair competition and which regulates an airline's communication with consumers can be construed as not "relating to prices, routes or services" under the ADA. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DAWN-SHEMAIN WEEKS et al VS. CMEA DEVELOPMENT COMPANY, LLC et al

The parties are to be prepared to address whether the confidentiality provision at par. 9(e) favors defendants and is substantively unconscionable. See Davis v. O'Melveny & Meyers, 485 F.3d 1066, 1079 (2007), overruled on another point in Kilgore v. KeyBank, Nat. Ass'n, 673 F.3d 947, 960 (9th Cir.2012)). =(302/MJM)
